For run_all: open a ThreadPoolExecutor with a 'with' block, then call pool.map(slow_task, tasks). Wrap the result in list() — pool.map returns an iterator.
pool.map preserves order automatically, so you don't need to sort or track indices.
For run_with_errors: build a dict mapping futures to task IDs — {pool.submit(slow_task, tid): tid for tid in tasks}.
Iterate over as_completed(future_to_id) to get each future as it finishes. Look up the task ID with future_to_id[future].
Inside the loop, call future.result() inside a try/except Exception block. On success, store the return value; on exception, store 'error'.
